svg.text: Components of the slide-then-tilt mechanism. All dimensions in mm.

g.text: A. Rail / Sled / Roller — cross-section (perpendicular to rack length)
Detail × 5 magnification. Looking down the long axis of the rack.
B. Pivot bracket at rear of slide rail
Side view. Rear roller drops into concave cradle when sled slid back.
Material: 6 mm MS plate, formed, hot-dip galvanised.
Concave radius 35 mm matches roller ø50 with 5 mm seat.
C. Slide latch — stow position lock
One per long side (port + stbd). Manual release lever; positive engagement.
Both port + stbd latches must be released before sliding.
Latches re-engage automatically when sled is slid forward to stow.
Simplified side view of rack front. Winch at front-base, cable runs up & rearward to sled front crossbar.
Winch position verified by moment analysis: cable from front-base to sled forward tip
provides correct lift rotation at all tilt angles (0° to 50°). Worst-case tension ≈ 640 kgf at horizontal.
E. IRB bow fastening — at sled rear crossbar
Holds IRB against gravity when sled is tilted. Required for safe operation.
Strap MUST be tensioned before tilt operation.
Strap MUST hold IRB if winch fails.
Inspect before each use — replace at first fray.
⚠ Always confirm strap engaged before lowering.
Top view of one sled assembly (port). The starboard sled is mirror image. Both linked by front + rear crossbars.

svg.text: All steel hot-dip galvanised per AS/NZS 4680. All hardware grade 8.8 or stainless 316 where in contact with seawater.
Quantity: 2 identical rack units. Fabricator confirms hardware sourcing and details before commit to fabrication.
